definition: A method or instrument for achieving a result.
example: We declare our right on this earth to be a man, to be a human being, to be respected as a human being—by any means necessary.

definition: Of or characteristic of people; claiming full personhood.
example: We declare our right on this earth to be a man, to be a human being, to be respected as a human being.

definition: Due regard for the feelings, rights, or traditions of others.
example: We declare our right on this earth to be a man, to be a human being, to be respected as a human being.

definition: A system of voting; political participation as power.
example: The ballot or the bullet—if you don't take it through the ballot, you may be forced to take it another way.

definition: A projectile from a firearm; a metaphor for force when politics fails.
The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction.
example: Education is the passport to the future, for tomorrow belongs to those who prepare for it today.

definition: An official document for travel; metaphorically, entry to opportunity.
example: Education is the passport to the future, for tomorrow belongs to those who prepare for it today.

definition: The day after today; the near future.
example: Education is the passport to the future, for tomorrow belongs to those who prepare for it today.

definition: That which is true or in accordance with fact.
example: I'm for truth, no matter who tells it. I'm for justice, no matter who it is for or against.
I'm for truth, no matter who tells it. I'm for justice, no matter who it is for or against.

definition: The quality of being free from pretense or deceit.
example: Usually when people are sad, they don't do anything. They just cry over their condition. But when they get angry, they bring about a change—sincerity without action is still sleep.

definition: Make or become different; social transformation.
example: Usually when people are sad, they don't do anything. They just cry over their condition. But when they get angry, they bring about a change.

definition: An association or community of people linked by common interest.
example: I believe in recognizing every human being as a human being—neither white, black, brown, or red; and when you are dealing with humanity as a family, brotherhood is not a slogan.

definition: A building for human habitation; metaphorically, the American order.
example: We are not fighting for integration, nor are we fighting for separation. We are fighting for recognition as human beings—and a house divided against itself still needs truth told inside it.

definition: To emerge or cause to emerge from sleep; to become alert.
example: The greatest mistake of the movement has been trying to organize a sleeping people around specific goals. You have to wake the people up first, then you'll get action.
